question: What material is used for the RPV shell, and what chemical composition limits apply to the beltline region according to Table 5.3-2?

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all predicted values remain within regulatory safety criteria.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for materials and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical pressure-retaining component that forms part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, supporting various internal systems and attachments, and is constructed and certified according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime. The materials used for the RPV and its components are carefully selected and specified to maintain structural integrity and comply with relevant ASME codes.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fastener requirements for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies procedures for maintaining vessel integrity during transport and operation, and lists the key codes and standards used for compliance.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the NuScale pressurizer and its components, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the specific alloys and material specifications for the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, heater sheaths, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing continuous monitoring and control.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document for regulatory purposes, and any copies must include the copyright notice. The section also references the table of contents for Chapter Five, detailing its structure and topics.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, detailing the integration of the reactor, steam generators, pressurizer, safety valves, and associated piping within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV). It outlines the configuration and boundaries of the steam generator system, emphasizing the integral design of the steam generators within the RPV and the separation of primary and secondary sides by specific components.

edges.description: Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with technical specifications for reactor vessel integrity, while Section B lists references, including material and chemistry guidelines, that inform those technical specifications and procedures.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with technical specifications for reactor vessel integrity, while Section B describes the physical components and materials of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV); both sections are connected through their focus on maintaining the integrity and safe operation of the RPV, but Section B provides the foundational material information that Section A's procedures are designed to protect.
Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that inform the material selection and chemical composition limits described in Section B for the RPV shell and beltline region.

Section A cites the ASME NQA-1-2008/1a-2009 Addenda as a quality assurance requirement, which is also referenced in Section B as the standard governing shipment, handling, and storage of the RPV, thus Section A provides a reference that Section B relies on for regulatory compliance.

Section A provides a general description of the RPV and its role in the reactor system, while Section B references specific standards and requirements (ASME BPVC and NQA-1) that apply to the RPV's shipment, handling, and storage, connecting the general component description to regulatory compliance details.

Section A references Section 5.2 for the material of the RPV upper shell, which is relevant to Section B's discussion of reactor vessel integrity and compliance with technical specifications, including chemical composition limits in the beltline region.

Section A refers to Section 5.2 for the material description of the RPV upper shell, while Section B lists references (such as EPRI and ASME standards) that likely contain the chemical composition limits applicable to the beltline region, thus Section A structurally references the more detailed requirements or standards found in Section B.

Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for the material description of the RPV upper shell, which is relevant to identifying the material used for the RPV shell as asked in the analysis question.

Section B provides references that may include standards or guidelines relevant to the chemical composition limits for materials used in the RPV shell, which is the subject of the analysis question addressed in Section A.

Section A references Section B by describing the pressurizer as including the top portion of the RPV upper shell and baffle plate, which are further detailed in Section B's description of the pressurizer as an integral part of the reactor vessel.

Section A provides the context and scope of the document, while Section B lists references that support or inform the technical content, such as material selection and chemical composition limits relevant to the RPV shell and beltline region.

Section A is a preface providing publication and copyright information for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, while Section B describes the reactor vessel materials and design context, with Section B's technical content being part of the document introduced by Section A.

Section A describes the components and design of the RPV, while Section B discusses procedural requirements to ensure the RPV operates within specified limits, connecting them through the operational compliance with the RPV's design and material specifications.

Section A describes the components and design of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B lists references that provide standards and guidelines relevant to the integrity and material requirements of the RPV, thus Section A's design is informed by or must comply with the standards referenced in Section B.

Section A provides a general description of the RPV and its components, while Section B gives more specific details about the RPV materials, thus Section B specifies the material details for the general RPV description in Section A.
